attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.criterion_name: End-to-end artifact correctness, reproducibility, and gating decision accuracy
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Attach/ensure cell-level spreadsheet formulas are visible (not only cached computed values) so reviewers can re-run calculations, and include an explicit deterministic rounding policy (or integer enforcement) in the memo. Also state explicitly whether mitigations were evaluated as separate scenarios or applied sequentially (the memo evaluates scenarios but should declare this assumption).
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts were produced and the memo documents the embedded-packet values (Q1..Q4) and the mitigation rates. The memo explicitly shows reproducible formulas (residual_PII = initial_PII*(1 - removal_rate); residual_relevant = initial_relevant*(1 - relevance_loss_rate); relevance_rate = residual_relevant/topk) and row-level calculations that match the embedded packet values and correct application of mitigation percentages (PII_filter: 90% PII removal & 8% relevant loss; metadata_scope: 60% PII removal & 2% relevant loss). Aggregate and per-query numbers in the memo are consistent (e.g., Q2 residual PII 0.3 and 1.2, relevance 64.4% and 68.6%). Sensitivity analysis and a clear, conservative final decision (hold/fail) are provided. The workbook file exists and the trace notes that formulas were cached into the OOXML; however the grader could not open cell-level formulas (openpyxl/unavailable) to fully verify that live spreadsheet formulas (not only cached values) are present. The memo documents fractional expected residuals and explicitly flags the missing rounding policy.

attempt_summary: {'baseline_relevance': 0.7, 'baseline_pii_hits': 9.0, 'best_mitigation': 'metadata_scope on relevance proximity, but it still fails the PII gate and does not release', 'combined_mitigation_relevance': 0.63112, 'combined_mitigation_pii': 0.36}

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include explicit workbook cell references or exported formula text in the memo (e.g., sheet and cell formulas) and an embedded table showing per-query post-mitigation expected PII and relevant_hits so the spreadsheet formulas can be re-run by reviewers. Attach a small CSV or plain-text reproduction of the workbook calculations to make formula verification possible in environments without openpyxl.
rubric_item_scores.notes: The memo lists the embedded packet (Q1..Q4) and reproduces baseline totals correctly (PII=9, relevant=28, topk=40, relevance=70%). Mitigation math applies the provided percentages correctly: PII_filter -> 10% PII remaining (9*0.1=0.9) and relevant 92% remaining (28*0.92=25.76); metadata_scope -> 40% PII remaining (9*0.4=3.6) and relevant 98% remaining (28*0.98=27.44); combined sequential effect correctly computed as 4% PII remaining (0.04*9=0.36) and 90.16% relevant remaining (0.9016*28=25.2448) yielding the reported relevance percentages. The memo documents validation checks, sensitivity analysis, rounding assumptions, and concludes a defensible 'Hold' because no scenario meets both binding thresholds (PII_hits==0 and relevance>=70%). The spreadsheet file is present per artifact-contract precheck but could not be opened in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so spreadsheet-formula verification was not performed; the memo contains explicit calculations and row-level source packet citations matching the embedded packet.
